---
title: "Biofortified Varieties - target agro-ecological zones"
format: html
bibliography: references.bib
---

```{r results='hide', warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}

library(tidyverse)
library(terra)
library(tidyterra)
library(readODS)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggtext)
library(tidyr)

```

# Load the Agro-Ecological Zones

```{r}
#| label: aez
#| message: false
#| warning: false

vect_aez_geo <- vect("spatial_data/IITA_AEZ_LGP_2020_SRTM_RCL_NGA_topo.topojson")
vect_aez_geo$AEZ <- as.factor(vect_aez_geo$AEZ)
levels(vect_aez_geo$AEZ) # Check levels
plot(vect_aez_geo, main = "Nigeria Agro-ecological zones based on 2020 LGP and elevation", levels=TRUE)

AEZ <- 1:7
AEZ_name <- c("Sahel Savanna", "Sudan Savanna", "Northern Guinea Savanna", "Southern Guinea Savanna", "Derived/Transition Savanna", "Forest", "Mid-Altitude")

df_aez_names <- tibble(AEZ, AEZ_name)
df_aez_names$AEZ <- as.factor(df_aez_names$AEZ)
levels(df_aez_names$AEZ) # Check levels

vect_aez_geo <- dplyr::left_join(vect_aez_geo, df_aez_names, by = c("AEZ") )

ggplot(vect_aez_geo)+
  geom_spatvector(aes(fill = AEZ_name)) +
  theme_minimal()

df_aez <- as.data.frame(vect_aez_geo)

```



# Load the Variety data

## Nigeria Seed Portal

```{r}
#| label: nsp_variety_data
#| message: false
#| warning: false

df_nsp <- read.csv("seedportal_varieties.csv")

str(df_nsp)
summary(df_nsp)

```

## Filter Bio-fortified Varieties

```{r}
#| label: filter_biofort
#| message: false
#| warning: false

df_nsp_biofort <- dplyr::filter(df_nsp, zinc == 1 |
                                  vit_A == 1 | iron == 1)

paste("Number of Bio-fortified varieties =", nrow(df_nsp_biofort))
biofort_crop_list <- as.list(unique(df_nsp_biofort$Crop.Name))


df_nsp_biofort_zinc <- dplyr::filter(df_nsp, zinc == 1)
paste("Number of varieties Bio-fortified with zinc =", nrow(df_nsp_biofort_zinc))

df_nsp_biofort_crop_zinc_sum <- df_nsp_biofort_zinc %>%
  group_by(Crop.Name) %>%
  summarise(across(
    c(
      n_guinea,
      s_guinea,
      sudan,
      derived,
      forest,
      mid_alt,
      lowland,
      sahel,
      all_zones
    ),
    sum,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ))

df_nsp_biofort_crop_zinc_sum |> print()

df_nsp_biofort_iron <- dplyr::filter(df_nsp, iron == 1)
paste("Number of varieties Bio-fortified with iron =", nrow(df_nsp_biofort_iron))

df_nsp_biofort_crop_iron_sum  <- df_nsp_biofort_iron %>%
  group_by(Crop.Name) %>%
  summarise(across(
    c(
      n_guinea,
      s_guinea,
      sudan,
      derived,
      forest,
      mid_alt,
      lowland,
      sahel,
      all_zones
    ),
    sum,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ))

df_nsp_biofort_crop_iron_sum |> print()

df_nsp_biofort_vit_A <- dplyr::filter(df_nsp, vit_A == 1)
paste("Number of varieties Bio-fortified with vitamin A =", nrow(df_nsp_biofort_vit_A))

df_nsp_biofort_crop_vit_A_sum <- df_nsp_biofort_vit_A %>%
  group_by(Crop.Name) %>%
  summarise(across(
    c(
      n_guinea,
      s_guinea,
      sudan,
      derived,
      forest,
      mid_alt,
      lowland,
      sahel,
      all_zones
    ),
    sum,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ))

df_nsp_biofort_crop_vit_A_sum |> print()

```

# Map the varieties in each AEZ

## Step 1: Reshape varieties data to long format by AEZ

```{r}

# Map AEZ classes to your column names
aez_map <- c(
  "1" = "sahel",
  "2" = "sudan",
  "3" = "n_guinea",
  "4" = "s_guinea",
  "5" = "derived",
  "6" = "forest",
  "7" = "mid_alt"
)

# For each AEZ class, count varieties per crop & micronutrient
# A variety "belongs" to an AEZ if its AEZ column value == 1

df_nsp_biofort_long <- df_nsp_biofort %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(sahel, sudan, n_guinea, s_guinea, derived, forest, mid_alt),
               names_to = "aez_name",
               values_to = "in_aez") %>%
  filter(in_aez == 1) %>%                         # keep only varieties present in that AEZ
  mutate(AEZ = as.character(match(aez_name,        # convert name back to AEZ class number
                                   c("sahel","sudan","n_guinea",
                                     "s_guinea","derived","forest","mid_alt"))))
```

## Step 2: Count varieties per Crop, Micronutrient, and AEZ

```{r}

micronutrients <- c("zinc", "iron", "vit_A")

df_nsp_biofort_counts <- df_nsp_biofort_long %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(micronutrients),
               names_to  = "micronutrient",
               values_to = "has_trait") %>%
  filter(has_trait == 1) %>%                       # keep only varieties with that micronutrient
  group_by(Crop.Name, micronutrient, AEZ) %>%
  summarise(n_varieties = n(), .groups = "drop")

```

##Step 3: Join to your SpatVector and plot

```{r}

# Join counts to spatial data
vect_aez_geo_joined <- vect_aez_geo %>%
  mutate(AEZ = as.character(AEZ)) %>%
  left_join(df_nsp_biofort_counts, by = "AEZ", relationship = "many-to-many")

# Plot: one facet per Crop x Micronutrient combination
ggplot(vect_aez_geo_joined) +
  geom_spatvector(data = vect_aez_geo, fill = "lightgrey", color = "lightgrey", linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_spatvector(aes(fill = n_varieties), color = "lightgrey", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "blues", na.value = "lightgrey",
                       name = "# Varieties") +
  facet_grid(micronutrient ~ Crop.Name) +
  labs(title = "Number of biofortified varieties per AEZ",
       caption = "Grey = no varieties for that crop/nutrient/AEZ combination") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text    = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid   = element_blank(),
    strip.text   = element_text(face = "bold", size = 8),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
```
